metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_332
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Kaeser et.al. provides evidence that ELKS2 is involved in synaptic vesicle priming at inhibitory synapses.

-In hippocampal slices, there is an increase in action potential-evoked release at inhibitory synapses. There is a small effect on release probability (as measured by paired pulse), this is not sufficient to explain the effect on evoked release. This indirectly suggests an increase of the primed vesicle pool (Figure 4).
-More directly, in hippocampal cultures, there is an increase in vesicle priming (measured with sucrose application, figure 7). This preparation shows an similar increase in action-potential evoked release (figure 6).

It should be noted that there is a complex interplay between ELKS1 and ELKS2 in vesicle priming, that is dependent on the type of synapses. In ELKS1/2 double knockouts, there is no effect on vesicle priming in inhibitory synapses (Liu et.al. 2014, Held et.al. 2016), but an decrease at excitatory synapses (Held et.al. 2016). The exact contribution of ELKS2 in vesicle priming has not been determined.. Experimental description: Mice were generated according to standard procedures (Ho et al., 2006 and Kaeser et al., 2008), targeting exon 3 of the ELKS2 gene by homologous recombination in R1 ES cells.
